SPY.notes: Massive V-reversal from gap-down lows. Opened near $639, ripped to $658+. Iran/Oman Hormuz diplomacy drove the reversal. Now consolidating near $656.

QQQ.notes: Recovered from -2.2% gap-down. Tech bouncing with broad market. TSLA still weak but NVDA, META recovering.

IWM.notes: Outperforming large caps today +1.4%. Domestic-oriented value bid continuing. Russell 2000 up +0.88% vs S&P -0.21% divergence.

vix.notes: Declining from morning spike. Down -3% from yesterday. March average was 25.56. Still elevated but risk premium fading on diplomacy hopes.

fear_greed.label: Extreme Fear
fear_greed.notes: Slightly higher than yesterday (14). Six of seven sub-indicators in extreme fear. Lowest levels since Nov 2025. Historically powerful contrarian bullish signal but muted by active war catalyst.

oil.notes: Massive $14 intraday range. Spiked on Trump rhetoric, pulled back on Iran/Oman diplomacy. April 6 Hormuz deadline is key binary event.

treasury_10y.notes: Rising on prolonged energy crisis inflation fears. March saw +37bps.

fed.notes: Zero rate cuts priced for rest of 2026. Oil/inflation fears killed cut expectations. Near 100% probability of continued pause.

options_flow.notes: Extreme put buying. Most bearish options positioning in weeks. Contrarian signal but unreliable with active war catalyst.

market_breadth.notes: More new lows than highs despite midday recovery. Internal weakness persists.

sector_performance.notes: Oil surge lifting energy. Tech lagging. Growth-to-value rotation in progress.
